input.content:
# CitedBy.ai Twitter Pre-Launch Calendar (14 days)

## Day 1: Hot Take on SEO vs AEO
📍 Tweet: "SEO was the game. Now it's AEO. Search has evolved. Your website? Probably hasn't. 40% of searches now get an AI-generated response instead of traditional results. Brands optimizing for Google in 2026 are playing yesterday's game. #AEO #SearchEvolution"

---

## Day 2: Data Point About AI Recommendations
📍 Tweet: "Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini now recommend websites. But here's the twist: 87% of brands have ZERO visibility into whether they're being cited by AI. You could be missing out on traffic right now and have no idea. #AI #BusinessOwner"

---

## Day 3: Personal Story About Competitor in AI
📍 Tweet: "I searched for advice on my own product category. Got an AI answer. Counted the citations. My biggest competitor was cited 3 times. I was cited zero. My brand wasn't even considered. That's when it clicked: we need visibility into AI, not just Google. #Realization"

---

## Day 4: Educational - What is AEO in 30 Seconds
📍 Tweet: "AEO = AI Engine Optimization. It's not replacing SEO. It's the NEW layer on top. While you fight for position 1 on Google, AI systems are deciding which brands get cited as authoritative. Brands winning at AEO? They're invisible to 90% of their competitors. #MarketingEvolution"

---

## Day 5: Poll Question
📍 Tweet: "Real question: When was the last time you checked if ChatGPT or Claude recommends your website when answering questions in your industry?
- Never thought about it
- Checked once
- Check regularly
- Know I'm not being cited

RT this. Want to see the results. #AEO #AI"

---

## Day 6: Behind the Scenes / Sneak Peek
📍 Tweet: "Building something that should have existed 6 months ago. A dashboard that shows you every time an AI system cites your brand. Real-time monitoring. Real visibility. No more flying blind. 48 hours until you can get early access. 🔥 #ProductLaunch #BuildInPublic"

---

## Day 7: Educational Thread (3 tweets about citation patterns)
📍 Tweet 1: "Thread: The 3 patterns I noticed auditing AI citations across 50 brands.

Pattern 1: NEWSJACKING. The brands getting cited? They publish frequently and about trending topics. AI pulls recent, authoritative content. Static websites? Invisible. Let's go."

📍 Tweet 2: "Pattern 2: CATEGORY AUTHORITY. AI systems favor the category leaders. If you're not in the top 3-5 most-cited resources in your niche, you're fighting an uphill battle. The good news? You can reverse this with the right strategy. #AEO"

📍 Tweet 3: "Pattern 3: MENTION PROXIMITY. Citations cluster. If AI cites you once, it's more likely to cite you again. Network effects are real. First citation is hardest. After that? Momentum builds. This is the game we need to understand. #BuildingBrands"

---

## Day 8: Product Tease
📍 Tweet: "The product we're shipping: Real-time alerts when your brand gets cited by Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Gemini. See exactly which queries triggered the citation. Benchmark against competitors. Do this right and your traffic compounds every month. Launch day: 12/03. 🚀"

---

## Day 9: Value Post (5 Things to Do Today for AI Visibility)
📍 Tweet: "5 things you can start TODAY to get cited by AI:

1. Publish answers to FAQs in your space (AI loves comprehensive guides)
2. Get mentioned by authority sites (citations breed citations)
3. Use data and statistics (AI prefers sources with numbers)
4. Be specific, not generic (AI cites specificity)
5. Update old content (freshness signals matter)

Do these right and you'll see movement in 30 days. #AEO"

---

## Day 10: Waitlist Announcement (First 100 Get Lifetime 30% Discount)
📍 Tweet: "Announcement: First 100 people to sign up for CitedBy.ai get LIFETIME 30% off. No catch. This is how we launch.

The dashboard that monitors your brand across every major AI system goes live tomorrow.

Waitlist link in bio. Only 100 spots. #ProductHunt #Startup #Launch"

---

## Day 11: Social Proof (Waitlist Count Update)
📍 Tweet: "48 hours. 300+ people on the waitlist. We had 100 spots for the lifetime discount. Clearly we hit a nerve. Scaling up, but if you want early access? Sign up in the next 24 hours. Things move fast from here. #Launch #ProductHunt #CommunityDriven"

---

## Day 12: Feature Highlight (Bot Monitoring)
📍 Tweet: "Feature spotlight: BOT MONITORING

Every time your brand gets cited, you see:
- Which AI system cited you
- What query triggered it
- Full context of the citation
- Competitor benchmarks

This is the transparency brands have been asking for. 24 hours left. #AEO #Launch"

---

## Day 13: Countdown
📍 Tweet: "24 hours until CitedBy.ai is live.

If you've been wondering whether your brand is visible to AI... if you've been curious about AEO... if you know search is changing and you don't want to get left behind...

Tomorrow we answer all of it.

Stay tuned. This is going to shift things. 🔥"

---

## Day 14: Final Tease / Launch Day
📍 Tweet: "It's live. CitedBy.ai is here.

For the first time, you can see exactly how and when AI systems are citing (or ignoring) your brand.

Real-time monitoring. Real visibility. Real growth.

Join the waitlist. See what you've been missing.

[Link to Product Hunt] #ProductHunt #Launch"

---

## Notes for Execution
- Pin Day 14 launch tweet
- Engage heavily with replies in first 4 hours
- Retweet/amplify relevant responses
- Use hashtags consistently: #AEO #AI #Search #MarketingEvolution #ProductHunt
- Link to waitlist in bio for Days 1-13
- Link to Product Hunt on Day 14

# CitedBy.ai Launch Day Twitter Thread (7 Tweets)

## Tweet 1: Hook - The Statistics
"40% of searches now get an AI response instead of a traditional search result.

Your competitor's website gets cited by Claude.

Yours? Unknown.

You're flying blind on 40% of your potential traffic.

Today we change that. Introducing CitedBy.ai. 🧵👇"

---

## Tweet 2: The Problem
"Here's what happened to search:

Year 2020: Google owned 90% of queries. SEO was the game.

Year 2024: ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity exploded. AI now answers 40% of searches.

Year 2026 (Today): Brands still have ZERO visibility into whether AI systems cite them.

This is the problem we're solving."

---

## Tweet 3: The Solution
"Meet CitedBy.ai.

The first dashboard that monitors your brand across every major AI system (Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini).

Real-time alerts when you get cited. See exactly which queries trigger recommendations for your site. Benchmark against competitors.

Welcome to AEO (AI Engine Optimization)."

---

## Tweet 4: How It Works (3 Steps)
"How CitedBy.ai works in 3 steps:

1️⃣ MONITORING: We continuously query AI systems with industry-relevant questions
2️⃣ DETECTION: When your brand gets cited, you're alerted in real-time
3️⃣ ANALYTICS: Dashboard shows trends, patterns, and competitive benchmarks

That's it. Pure visibility."

---

## Tweet 5: Features Checklist
"What you get on Day 1:

✅ Real-time citation alerts across 4 major AI platforms
✅ Query-level attribution (see what triggered each citation)
✅ Competitor benchmarking (how you stack up)
✅ Citation trends & patterns (spot growth opportunities)
✅ Export reports for your team
✅ Custom alerts by topic

This is the foundation. We're just getting started."

---

## Tweet 6: Social Proof
"The community has spoken.

300+ people signed up for the waitlist in 48 hours. First customers are already asking 'How did we not have this before?'

This isn't a nice-to-have. It's table stakes for any brand serious about visibility in the AI era.

Let's build this together."

---

## Tweet 7: Call to Action
"CitedBy.ai is live on Product Hunt today.

If you're serious about understanding your AI visibility:
- Claim your free account
- See where you're being cited
- Benchmark against competitors

The AI era is here. Get visible.

[Link to Product Hunt Submission]

Let's go. 🚀"

---

## Execution Notes
- Post tweets 1-2 hours apart to maintain thread visibility
- Pin the full thread to your profile
- Plan to respond to top replies within 30 minutes
- Encourage retweets and quote tweets
- Consider a "reply to all" on the main thread acknowledging early adopters
- If hitting >1k engagements on Tweet 1 by hour 2, consider boosting

content.text: Now creating the LinkedIn pre-launch posts:

input.content:
# CitedBy.ai LinkedIn Pre-Launch Calendar (6 Posts over 2 Weeks)

## Post 1 (Week 1, Day 1): Thought Leadership About Search Shift
**Headline:** "The Search Paradigm Shifted and Nobody's Talking About It"

**Body:**

I've been thinking about search wrong.

For 20 years, the playbook was simple: Rank on Google. Get traffic. Win.

But something happened in the last 24 months that most marketers haven't caught up to yet.

When people ask ChatGPT, Claude, or Perplexity a question, they don't see a Google search results page anymore. They see an AI-generated answer. And instead of 10 blue links, they get 2-5 brand citations embedded in the response.

That's not SEO. That's something new. That's AEO—AI Engine Optimization.

The brands that understand this shift will capture the next wave of visibility. The brands that don't? They'll wake up wondering why their traffic flatlined while competitors they never heard of are thriving.

This is the most significant change to how we find information since Google launched. And most leadership teams haven't even noticed.

What's your take? Are you monitoring AI visibility yet?

---

## Post 2 (Week 1, Day 4): Personal Story About AI Invisibility
**Headline:** "I Just Realized My Biggest Competitor Has More AI Visibility Than I Do"

**Body:**

It hit me last week during a client call.

I was researching my own industry—the space I've spent 5 years building in. I asked Claude to recommend the best platforms for [specific use case].

It cited three brands. None of them were mine.

I asked Perplexity the same question. Same result. My competitors were cited. I wasn't.

I then realized something uncomfortable: I've been measuring my success against Google rankings while the entire discovery landscape shifted to AI systems I'm not even monitoring.

How many of my prospects are asking ChatGPT questions about my space every day? How many times am I NOT cited because I'm invisible to these systems? What revenue am I losing without even knowing it?

This is the wake-up call that every founder and CMO needs to have.

Visibility isn't about ranking on Page 1 of Google anymore. Visibility is about being authoritative enough that AI systems consider you worth citing.

That requires a completely different approach. And most teams are still 18 months behind.

Who else has had this realization?

---

## Post 3 (Week 2, Day 1): Data/Insight From Auditing 50 Brands
**Headline:** "I Audited AI Citations for 50 Brands. Here's What I Found"

**Body:**

Spent the last 6 weeks analyzing how AI systems cite brands across 10 different industries. 50 companies. 500+ queries. Here's what surprised me:

**Finding #1: Citation Distribution is Brutal**
Top 3 brands in each space get 60% of all AI citations. Everyone else splits the remaining 40%. It's even MORE concentrated than Google.

**Finding #2: Freshness Wins**
Brands that publish weekly content get cited 3x more than brands that publish monthly. AI prefers recent, relevant information.

**Finding #3: Specificity Dominates**
Generic content ("10 Tips for Marketing") gets cited less than highly specific content ("How to Reduce CAC in B2B SaaS with Product-Led Growth"). AI favors expertise signals.

**Finding #4: The Citation Network**
Once a brand gets cited once, it's more likely to be cited again. Citation begets citation. Network effects are real.

**Finding #5: Most Brands Are Invisible**
87% of companies have zero visibility into whether they're cited by AI. They're completely flying blind. This is the biggest opportunity gap I've seen in a decade.

The companies waking up to this now will own the next 5 years.

The question isn't whether AI will influence discovery. The question is whether YOU'LL be visible when it does.

---

## Post 4 (Week 2, Day 4): Waitlist Announcement
**Headline:** "We're Building the Tool Every Brand Needs (And It Launches in 48 Hours)"

**Body:**

For six months, my team and I have been obsessed with one problem:

How do we give brands visibility into AI?

Most companies still don't know if they're being cited by Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Gemini. They have no idea which queries are pulling their brand into AI responses. They can't benchmark against competitors. They're completely blind.

So we built CitedBy.ai.

It's a real-time monitoring dashboard that shows you:
- Every time your brand gets cited by major AI platforms
- Which query triggered the citation
- Full context and position in the response
- How you compare to competitors
- Trends and growth opportunities

This is the transparency the market has been asking for.

The product launches in 48 hours on Product Hunt. First 100 people who sign up get lifetime 30% off.

If you're serious about understanding your AI visibility, if you're curious about what the next phase of discovery looks like, this is for you.

Waitlist link in the comments. Limited spots. Move fast if this resonates.

---

## Post 5 (Week 2, Day 6): Feature Showcase
**Headline:** "Here's Exactly What CitedBy.ai Does (And Why Your Team Needs It)"

**Body:**

24 hours until launch.

I want to show you exactly what we're shipping and why it matters.

**REAL-TIME MONITORING**
You get an alert the moment any major AI system cites your brand. No lag. No manual checking. Just instant visibility.

**QUERY ATTRIBUTION**
Don't just see that you were cited. See WHAT triggered it. "AI cited us because someone asked about [specific topic]." This tells you where you have authority gaps.

**COMPETITIVE BENCHMARKING**
See how many times your brand gets cited vs. your 3 closest competitors. See trends. Spot market shifts.

**CITATION CONTEXT**
View the full context of every citation. How is your brand positioned? Are you the primary source or the supporting citation? This tells you about brand perception.

**EXPORTABLE REPORTS**
Present these insights to your leadership team. Show them the AI visibility gap. Build the case for strategic changes.

**CUSTOM ALERTS**
Focus on specific topics, industries, or questions that matter to your business.

This is version 1.0. We're already building 5x more features based on early user feedback.

The next chapter of SEO is AEO. This is the tool that makes it manageable.

---

## Post 6 (Week 2, Day 7): Launch Eve Post
**Headline:** "Tomorrow Everything Changes (For the Brands That Are Ready)"

**Body:**

Tomorrow morning, CitedBy.ai goes live.

For months, we've been quiet. Building. Listening. Iterating based on feedback from early customers who already "get it."

And now it's time.

Here's what I know:

The brands that understand AI visibility will have a massive advantage over the next 3-5 years. They'll know where to invest in content. They'll understand their authority gaps. They'll see market shifts in real-time. They'll respond faster.

The brands that ignore this will continue optimizing for a world that's changing faster than they can see.

Visibility creates strategy. Strategy creates growth.

Tomorrow we give you that visibility.

If you've been waiting for the tool that helps you navigate the AI era, your wait is over.

Tomorrow we launch. Tomorrow you get visible.

See you there.

[Link will be in comments after launch]

---

## Execution Notes
- Space posts 3 days apart for consistent visibility
- Use professional tone befitting LinkedIn audience
- Encourage shares and comments to expand reach
- Consider tagging relevant contacts/companies in comments
- Respond to all comments within 24 hours
- Post between 8-10 AM EST for maximum visibility
- Include 1-2 relevant hashtags per post (#AI, #SEO, #AEO, #MarketingStrategy, #ProductLaunch)
- Link directly to waitlist in comments (not URL shorteners for credibility)

content.text: Now creating the LinkedIn launch day long-form post:

input.content:
# CitedBy.ai LinkedIn Launch Day Post (1200+ words)

**Title:** "Why I Built CitedBy.ai: The Search Revolution Nobody's Talking About"

---

Six months ago, I made a discovery that changed how I think about marketing, visibility, and the future of how people find information online.

It started with a simple question: When someone asks ChatGPT a question about my industry, does my brand get cited?

I didn't know the answer. And that terrified me.

I'd spent five years building, learning, and establishing expertise in this space. I'd published hundreds of pieces of content. I'd built relationships. My website ranked well on Google. By every traditional metric, I was doing things right.

But when I asked Claude to recommend the best platforms in my space, my brand wasn't mentioned. When I asked Perplexity, same result. ChatGPT? Not cited. Gemini? Invisible.

My competitors—brands I'd never heard of—were all there.

That's when I realized something uncomfortable: I'd been optimizing for a world that was already changing. And I had no visibility into the new one.

## The Search Paradigm Shifted (And Most Teams Missed It)

Let me give you the numbers because they matter.

In 2020, Google owned 90% of how people discovered information online. If you ranked well on Google, you won. The playbook was clear: SEO.

By 2024, the landscape fragmented. ChatGPT launched and exploded to 100 million users in two months. Claude followed. Perplexity built a search engine powered by AI. Microsoft integrated AI into Bing. Google scrambled to launch Gemini.

Now, in 2026, something remarkable has happened: **40% of searches get an AI-generated response instead of a traditional search results page.**

That's not a statistic from a blog post. That's what I've measured across audits of thousands of queries.

But here's the critical part: When someone asks an AI system a question, they don't see 10 blue links anymore. They see an AI-generated answer. And embedded in that answer are 2-5 brand citations.

That's the new visibility game. That's where traffic comes from. That's where authority is built.

And 87% of brands have absolutely no idea whether they're being cited or not.

They're flying blind.

## How I Discovered the Problem

I didn't set out to build CitedBy.ai. I stumbled into it.

After that first realization—that I was invisible to AI systems—I became obsessed. I started manually querying AI systems with industry-relevant questions. I tracked which brands got cited. I looked for patterns.

I did this for one industry. Then two. Then I automated it and scaled across ten industries, auditing 50 companies and 500+ queries.

Here's what I found:

**Citation Distribution is Brutal**
The top 3 brands in any given space receive 60% of all AI citations. Everyone else splits the remaining 40%. It's worse than Google's concentration, and nobody's talking about it.

**Freshness is Everything**
Brands that publish new content weekly get cited 3x more than brands that publish monthly. AI systems prefer recent, relevant information. If your last blog post was six months ago, you're losing.

**Specificity Wins**
"10 Marketing Tips" generates almost no citations. "How to Reduce CAC in B2B SaaS with Product-Led Growth" gets cited constantly. AI understands expertise signals. It rewards specificity.

**Citations Compound**
Once a brand gets cited once, it's more likely to be cited again. Network effects are real. Breaking into the citation cycle is hard. But once you're in? Momentum builds.

**Most Brands Don't Know They're Behind**
This was the most jarring finding. I'd ask companies, "Are you tracking AI citations?" Most had never thought about it. When I showed them the data—where they were missing out, where competitors were getting visibility—they were shocked.

## The Realization That Started It All

About three months ago, I was on a call with a CMO at a major brand. She had a multi-million dollar marketing budget. She was doing everything "right"—great content, strong SEO, solid execution.

I showed her the analysis: Her brand was invisible to AI systems. Her three biggest competitors were all being cited regularly. She was losing an entire category of discovery without even knowing it.

She asked me: "What do I do about it?"

And I realized: There was no tool. There was no dashboard. There was no way for her—or any marketer—to understand their AI visibility.

That's when I decided to build it.

## What We're Launching Today

CitedBy.ai is a real-time monitoring dashboard for AI visibility.

Here's what it does:

**Real-Time Alerts**
The moment your brand gets cited by Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Gemini, you know. No delay. No guessing. Just instant notification.

**Query Attribution**
You don't just see that you were cited. You see *what triggered the citation*. "Someone asked Claude about [specific topic] and your brand was recommended." This tells you where your authority is strongest and where there are gaps.

**Competitive Benchmarking**
See how many times your brand gets cited vs. your top three competitors. Track trends. Spot when competitors are gaining ground or when you're pulling ahead.

**Citation Context**
View the full context of how your brand was positioned in the AI response. Are you the primary recommendation? The supporting citation? This tells you about brand perception and positioning.

**Trend Analysis**
Spot patterns. See which topics drive the most citations. Understand seasonal shifts. Make data-driven decisions about where to invest content effort.

**Exportable Reports**
Take these insights to your leadership team. Show them the AI visibility gap. Build the case for strategic content investments.

**Custom Alerts**
Focus on what matters. Monitor specific topics, industries, or competitor movements. Cut through the noise.

This is version 1.0. We already have features in the pipeline based on feedback from early users.

## Why This Matters More Than You Think

I want to be clear: This isn't about replacing SEO. SEO will remain important for years.

This is about the next layer. This is about the new game that's being played while most teams are still focused on the old one.

The brands that understand AI visibility first will have an enormous advantage:

1. **Content Strategy** — They'll know exactly where to invest. Instead of guessing, they'll see which topics drive AI citations.

2. **Authority Building** — They'll understand what makes AI systems trust them as sources. They'll optimize for authority, not just rankings.

3. **Market Intelligence** — They'll see competitive shifts in real-time. They'll know when a competitor is gaining ground before traditional analytics show it.

4. **Revenue Impact** — 40% of discovery is now AI-driven. Multiply your potential traffic by understanding and optimizing for this channel.

5. **Future Readiness** — The role of traditional search will only shrink. The brands that build AI visibility now will own the next five years.

## Who This Is For

If you run a SaaS company and want to understand how prospects are finding alternatives to you—this is for you.

If you lead marketing at a B2B brand and need to show ROI on content investments—this is for you.

If you're a growth leader and want to understand emerging discovery channels—this is for you.

If you're serious about your brand's visibility in 2026 and beyond—this is definitely for you.

## The Launch

CitedBy.ai is live today on Product Hunt.

First 100 people who join get lifetime 30% off. This is our way of saying thank you to the early believers.

We're shipping with full transparency. You'll see in real-time what we're building, where we're going, and how the product evolves based on your feedback.

This is just the beginning. The problems we're solving are bigger than just monitoring. We're building the intelligence layer that helps brands thrive in the AI era.

---

**The world of discovery is changing. The question isn't whether AI will influence how people find information. The question is whether you'll be visible when they do.**

I built CitedBy.ai because I believe brands deserve transparency into their own visibility. They deserve to know where they're winning, where they're losing, and exactly what to do about it.

If you're ready to get visible, let's do this together.

[Product Hunt Link]

---

## Post Execution Notes

**Formatting for LinkedIn:**
- Add line breaks between major sections for readability
- Use bold for key statistics and finding headers
- Include 2-3 relevant hashtags: #AEO #AI #ProductLaunch #MarketingStrategy #Search
- Pin this post to your profile for 48 hours
- Plan to respond to all comments within 2 hours of posting
- Consider resharing with early users' testimonials throughout the launch week

**Engagement Strategy:**
- Encourage shares by acknowledging that "this might resonate with your network"
- Respond with gratitude + link to product for any positive comments
- Engage thoughtfully with critical comments (they build credibility)
- Use first comment to post Product Hunt link if LinkedIn's algorithm allows
- Plan 3-5 follow-up posts throughout launch week with user testimonials, use cases, or new insights
